steps.title: Distribute the 3
steps.content: Start with $$3(x+5)-6$$ Distribute $3$ to both terms inside the parentheses: $$3x+15-6$$

steps.title: Combine constants
steps.content: The constant terms are $15$ and $-6$: $$15-6=9$$ So the expression simplifies to $$3x+9$$

steps.title: Avoid the common distribution error
steps.content: The $3$ multiplies both $x$ and $5$, giving $3x+15$ before subtracting $6$. Do not combine the $5$ and $-6$ before distributing.
